title: Session 3 — Cognitive Restructuring Introduction
content:
## Clinical Session

**Date:** July 12, 2026
**Duration:** 60 min
**Type:** Individual

### Opening / Check-in

Jose arrived on time. Reports completing all homework. Mood continues gradual improvement. Denied crisis events. Sertraline 50 mg continued — no side effects. No alcohol use this week. Sleep: early-morning awakening reduced to 1-2 times per week. Appetite: improved, gaining weight back.

### Mental Status Exam

- **Appearance:** Well-groomed, brighter affect
- **Behavior:** Cooperative, good eye contact, normal motor activity
- **Speech:** Normal rate, rhythm, volume, and prosody
- **Mood:** "Better — maybe 30% improved"
- **Affect:** Fuller range, reactive, congruent
- **Thought Process:** Linear and goal-directed
- **Thought Content:** Less negative self-focus. Denies passive death ideation. No SI/HI.
- **Cognition:** Intact
- **Insight/Judgment:** Good insight — recognizing patterns between activity and mood.

### Symptom Review

- Level 2 Depression (PROMIS) follow-up: T-score 60 (moderate) — down from 66. 6-point improvement.
- Level 2 Anxiety (PROMIS) follow-up: T-score 56 (moderate) — down from 62. 6-point improvement.
- Activity log: Completed 6/7 days. Average 5 activities/day (up from 3).
- Relaxation practice: Completed 5/7 days. Reports noticeable calming effect.
- Somatic symptoms: Reduced. Headaches less frequent. Self-report discrepancy with clinician rating narrowing.

### Safety Screening

- Passive death ideation: Absent x 2 weeks.
- C-SSRS: Not administered (no trigger).
- Safety plan: Not due for review until week 4.
- Crisis resources: No changes.

### Interventions Delivered

- Modality: CBT — Cognitive Restructuring phase (week 3-8) + Behavioral Activation continuation
- Reviewed activity monitoring — praised consistent adherence, discussed expanding activities
- Introduced cognitive restructuring: thought record, identifying automatic thoughts, cognitive distortions overview
- Assigned thought record for the coming week: capture 3 negative automatic thoughts per day
- Continued diaphragmatic breathing — reviewed technique, encouraged daily practice
- Introduced progressive muscle relaxation (PMR) — practiced in session, assigned 3x/week
- Discussed spouse involvement: patient open to bringing spouse to session 5 for communication skills

### Patient Response

- Emotional response: Engaged, motivated. Expressed interest in "understanding my thinking patterns."
- Cognitive engagement: Quick grasp of thought record concept. Identified examples of all-or-nothing thinking.
- Barriers: Concerned about consistency with thought records. Problem-solved: set phone reminders.
- Within-session change: Relaxation visible after PMR practice.

### Session Plan for Next Visit

**Date:** July 19, 2026
**Focus:** Review thought records, identify core cognitive distortions, introduce cognitive restructuring techniques. Continue behavioral activation. Review somatic symptom log.

### Progress Notes

Session 3 shows continued improvement with T-score reductions on both depression (-6) and anxiety (-6). Passive death ideation resolved for 2 consecutive weeks. Behavioral activation showing objective gains (activity count 3→5/day). Transitioning to cognitive restructuring phase per protocol. Patient responding well to relaxation training. Spouse involvement planned for session 5.
